data.description:
## Why / Summary

Outbound comment sync is non-idempotent: `get_comments()` fetches only the FIRST
PAGE of a Jira issue's comments, so the dedup in `_diff_comments` re-posts every
local comment outside that page on every reconcile pass. Over time this inflated 13
Jira issues to the 5000-comment hard cap (local truth: 22-76 comments each); once
capped, further comment adds fail `COMMENTS_PER_ISSUE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` â€” and because
acli returns exit 0 on that FAILURE, the failure is swallowed as a silent no-op.

## Root cause (proven)

`get_comments()` in BOTH `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_cli_ops.py:516`
and `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli.py:653` invokes
`acli jira workitem comment list --key <k> --json` with NO `--paginate` and NO
`--limit`. acli defaults to `--limit 50`, `--order +created` (oldest first), so the
call returns only the 50 OLDEST comments â€” despite both docstrings claiming "Get all
comments." Runtime-verified against REB-155: response `{isLast:false, maxResults:50,
startAt:0, total:5000}`, 50 returned. `_diff_comments`
(`outbound_comments.py`) then compares each local comment against only those 50
bodies and re-emits any not present as an "add".

## Reproduction Steps

1. A Jira issue bound to a rebar ticket has > 50 comments, and the local ticket's
   comments are not all within the oldest-50 page.
2. Run a reconcile pass (or call `_diff_comments` with a live client whose
   `get_comments` returns page 1 only).
3. Observe: `_diff_comments` emits "add" mutations for local comments already present
   in Jira (beyond page 1) â€” Expected: 0 adds for an already-mirrored ticket.

- Expected: a steady-state pass emits **0** comment "add" mutations for a ticket
  whose comments are already mirrored to Jira, regardless of Jira comment count.
- Actual: it re-emits the comments not on Jira's oldest-50 page every pass.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `get_comments()` returns the COMPLETE comment set for an issue (paginates all
      pages), in both `acli_cli_ops.py` and `acli.py`. Proving command:
      a unit test builds a fake acli that returns 3 pages (e.g. 50+50+20=120) and
      asserts `get_comments` returns all 120 â€” fails on the current single-page code.
- [ ] `_diff_comments` emits 0 "add" mutations when every local comment is already
      present in Jira but only beyond page 1. Proving command: unit test with a
      >50-comment Jira snapshot where the matching local comment is comment #75 â€”
      asserts empty mutation list; RED on current code.
- [ ] The full reconciler unit suite passes: `make test` (or
      `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler -q`).
- [ ] No new external dependency introduced; change is confined to the two
      `get_comments` call sites (+ tests).

## Notes

Fix = add `--paginate` (acli ignores `--limit` when paginating) OR loop startAt
until `isLast`. Prefer `--paginate` for parity with `acli`'s own pagination.
This is P1 of epic 58b0. The 13 capped Jira issues need a separate data cleanup
(sibling ticket) AFTER this fix deploys, else the buggy reconciler re-inflates them.
data.title: get_comments fetches only page 1 -> non-idempotent comment re-posting inflates Jira to 5000-comment cap
